Scenario.title: Energy Sector Cyber Espionage Campaign C0027
Scenario.overview: Energy Complex A sophisticated cyber attack targeting energy sector infrastructure utilizing multi-stage exploitation of industrial control systems, leveraging AI-driven reconnaissance, custom tool development, and lateral movement techniques to achieve persistent access and operational disruption.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Open Source Intelligence (OSINT), Process, OS Process, Windows Registry, Application Log, Network Traffic, Domain Name Registrar, File, Malware Artifact, Public IP/Network Block
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Artificial Intelligence, Process Creation, General Access Method, Code Injection, Windows Registry Key Creation, Application Log Content, Process Injection, Network Traffic Flow, Pass the Hash, File Modification, Encrypted Channel, Disabling Security Tools

stage.name: Reconnaissance
stage.description: The attacker uses AI algorithms to analyze publicly available data sources, identifying vulnerabilities in energy sector infrastructure and personnel schedules to plan targeted attacks.

stage.name: Resource Development
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Custom malware development tools are created to exploit industrial control systems, enabling precise manipulation of SCADA protocols and bypassing standard security measures.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: Spear-phishing campaigns deliver stripped payloads to employee credentials, exploiting weak email security to establish initial footholds in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Malicious code is injected into trusted processes to execute payload without triggering traditional detection mechanisms.

stage.name: Persistence
stage.description: A scheduled task is created in the Windows Registry to maintain persistence across reboots, ensuring continuous access to compromised systems.

stage.name: Privilege Escalation
stage.description: Unpatched SCADA application logs are exploited to escalate privileges, granting administrative access to critical infrastructure components.

stage.name: Defense Evasion
stage.description: Malicious processes are injected into legitimate system processes to evade detection by security monitoring tools.

stage.name: Credential Access
stage.capec: CAPEC-670: Software Development Tools Maliciously Altered
stage.description: Stolen credentials are extracted from memory using altered deployment tools, allowing access to additional systems within the network.

stage.name: Discovery
stage.description: Network traffic analysis identifies critical systems and pathways, enabling targeted exploitation of high-value targets.

stage.name: Lateral Movement
stage.capec: CAPEC-461: Web Services API Signature Forgery Leveraging Hash Function Extension Weakness
stage.description: Stolen credentials are used to move laterally across the network, compromising additional systems and expanding the attack surface.

stage.name: Collection
stage.capec: CAPEC-675: Retrieve Data from Decommissioned Devices
stage.description: Sensitive operational data is exfiltrated by modifying and extracting files from decommissioned systems, ensuring data retention despite cleanup efforts.

stage.name: Exfiltration
stage.description: Data is transferred out of the network via encrypted channels embedded in malware artifacts, evading traditional network monitoring tools.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Security tools are disabled to prevent detection, allowing the attackers to cause physical disruptions to energy operations through targeted system tampering.

Scenario.summary: The C0027 campaign demonstrates advanced persistence and evasion techniques, combining AI-driven reconnaissance with custom tool development to infiltrate and disrupt energy sector infrastructure. The attack chain emphasizes long-term operational control and data exfiltration while avoiding detection through multiple layers of obfuscation and privilege escalation.
